categories.name: Migration Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing autonomous, idempotent, CI-only schema migrations

categories.name: Frontend Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing frontend structure, navigation, and security

categories.name: CI/CD Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing CI/CD workflow standards and naming conventions

categories.name: Database Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing database security, RLS, and tenant isolation

categories.name: Agent Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing autonomous agent behavior and operational protocols

categories.name: API Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing API standards, traceability, and health monitoring

categories.name: Task Discovery Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing OASIS as the single source of truth for task state

categories.name: Memory Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing memory data privacy, access control, and portability

categories.name: System Controls Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing audit trails and access control for system control operations

categories.name: Spec Governance
categories.description: Rules enforcing immutability and integrity of VTID specifications

rules.title: Idempotent SQL Requirement
rules.description: All migrations MUST use idempotent SQL patterns (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, DROP POLICY IF EXISTS, CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS, INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) to allow safe re-runs via CI.

rules.title: CI-Only Migration Execution
rules.description: All schema migrations MUST run exclusively through the canonical GitHub Actions workflow (APPLY-MIGRATIONS.yml). No direct SQL execution via Supabase UI, local CLI, or Cloud Shell is allowed.

rules.title: No Manual SQL
rules.description: Manual SQL changes (in Supabase Dashboard, local psql, or Cloud Shell) are prohibited. Any schema modification outside CI MUST be rejected by governance and Validator.

rules.title: Mandatory CI Failure on Migration Errors
rules.description: The migration workflow MUST fail (non-zero exit) on any SQL error or verification error. Silent failures or partial application of migrations are not allowed.

rules.title: Use Only Existing Secrets
rules.description: Migration workflows MUST use only existing, approved secrets (e.g., SUPABASE_DB_URL). Introducing new credentials or ad-hoc connection strings is forbidden.

rules.title: Tenant Isolation Enforcement
rules.description: All governance-related schema objects MUST include tenant-aware design (tenant_id and appropriate RLS) to preserve tenant isolation across the Vitana platform.

rules.title: Timestamp-Ordered Migrations
rules.description: All migration files MUST follow the global timestamp naming convention (YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_description.sql), and CI MUST apply them in sorted order to guarantee deterministic schema evolution.

rules.title: Frontend Canonical Source
rules.description: Only one valid source tree for the Command Hub is allowed: services/gateway/src/frontend/command-hub. Forbidden paths include static/command-hub, public/command-hub, frontend/command-hub, and any variant casing. CI and Validator block violations.

rules.title: Navigation Canon
rules.description: The frontend navigation structure is fixed with exactly 17 modules and 87 screens in a canonical order. This structure MUST match the OASIS spec in specs/dev_screen_inventory_v1.json. Modifications require OASIS spec update first.

rules.title: CSP Compliance
rules.description: All frontend routes MUST set Content-Security-Policy headers. No inline scripts or styles allowed. CSP: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'.

rules.title: Workflow UPPERCASE Naming
rules.description: All GitHub Actions workflow files MUST use UPPERCASE names with hyphens (e.g., DEPLOY-GATEWAY.yml, RUN-TESTS.yml). Reusable workflows prefixed with underscore (_) are exempt.

rules.title: Workflow VTID in run-name
rules.description: All workflows SHOULD include VTID reference in run-name field for tracking (e.g., run-name: 'Deploy Gateway [VTID: DEV-CICDL-0031] (${{ github.ref_name }})').

rules.title: File Naming Convention (kebab-case)
rules.description: All TypeScript/JavaScript code files MUST use kebab-case naming (e.g., my-service.ts). Exceptions: README, LICENSE, CHANGELOG, Dockerfile, Makefile.

rules.title: Service Manifest Required
rules.description: Every service directory (agents, MCP services, gateway, deploy-watcher) MUST contain a manifest.json with required fields: name, and either vtid or vt_layer/vt_module.

rules.title: Top-Level Service Directory Naming
rules.description: Top-level service directories MUST use kebab-case. Internal subdirectories may follow language conventions (e.g., Python snake_case).

rules.title: OpenAPI Spectral Validation
rules.description: All OpenAPI specification files in specs/ and packages/openapi/ MUST pass Spectral validation with fail-severity=warn.

rules.title: OpenAPI Version Requirement
rules.description: All OpenAPI specs MUST use version 3.0.x or 3.1.x. Older versions are not supported.

rules.title: No Duplicate Operation IDs
rules.description: Each OpenAPI operation MUST have a unique operationId within a specification. Duplicate operationIds are not allowed.

rules.title: Prisma Schema Check
rules.description: Prisma schema MUST pass format --check validation. Schema generation and formatting are verified in CI.

rules.title: RLS Enabled on Governance Tables
rules.description: Row Level Security MUST be enabled on all governance tables: governance_categories, governance_rules, governance_evaluations, governance_violations, governance_enforcements.

rules.title: Service Role Write Access
rules.description: Write access to governance tables is restricted to service_role only. Backend services use service_role for all write operations.

rules.title: Authenticated Read Access
rules.description: Authenticated users have read-only access to governance tables for transparency.

rules.title: OASIS Events Tenant Isolation
rules.description: OasisEvent table has RLS enabled with tenant-aware reads. Users can only SELECT events matching their JWT tenant claim.

rules.title: OASIS Events Service Insert Only
rules.description: Only service_role can INSERT into OasisEvent table. Gateway backend uses service_role for event ingestion.

rules.title: VtidLedger RLS Policies
rules.description: VtidLedger table has RLS enabled. Authenticated users can read all VTIDs, insert new VTIDs, and update only their tenant's VTIDs (or if admin).

rules.title: Claude Operational Protocol (COP)
rules.description: Claude operates as Chief Autonomous Execution Officer under CEO/CTO governance. All tasks must honor OASIS as Single Source of Truth and preserve deterministic reproducibility.

rules.title: VTID Required for All Tasks
rules.description: Every task executed by Claude or agents MUST include a VTID in its header (e.g., DEV-COMMU-0050). Tasks without VTID are invalid.

rules.title: No Direct Push to Main
rules.description: No direct pushes to main branch unless CEO explicitly orders. All changes must go through a PR with structured body including Summary, Context, Implementation details, Validation evidence, and OASIS event reference.

rules.title: Command Hierarchy
rules.description: Command hierarchy must be respected: CEO (Ultimate authority) > CTO/OASIS (Governance layer) > Claude (Executor) > Gemini/Worker Agents > Validator Agents. Claude never overrides CEO or OASIS directives.

rules.title: Exact-Match Edit Protocol
rules.description: Claude must verify exact target snippet exists before modification. If snippet not found: STOP immediately, report mismatch, do not improvise or guess. Escalate to CEO for correction.

rules.title: Telemetry Event Emission
rules.description: Every execution must emit a telemetry event to OASIS including: service name, VTID, start/end timestamps, outcome (success/warning/failure), and files touched.

rules.title: Safety and Validation Framework
rules.description: Claude must: validate all JSON/YAML schemas before commit, never expose secrets/API keys, treat OASIS/Gateway/Supabase credentials as secure, default to read-only unless explicitly instructed.

rules.title: VTID Required in API Requests
rules.description: API requests requiring traceability MUST include VTID via X-VTID header, body parameter, or query parameter. Middleware enforces this requirement.

rules.title: Health Endpoint Requirement
rules.description: All deployed services MUST expose health endpoints (/alive, /healthz, or /health) that return 200 status. CI deployment workflows verify health after deploy.

rules.title: Deployment Version Recording
rules.description: All deployments MUST record software version in OASIS via /api/v1/operator/deployments endpoint including: service name, git commit, deploy type, initiator, and environment.

rules.title: OASIS_ONLY_TASK_TRUTH
rules.description: All task state queries MUST source from OASIS. Non-OASIS sources are blocked.

rules.title: RLS on Memory Governance Tables
rules.description: RLS enabled on memory_visibility_prefs, memory_locks, memory_deletions, memory_exports with user+tenant isolation.

rules.title: User-Only Memory Access
rules.description: Users can only access their own memory governance settings. RLS enforces user_id = auth.uid() AND tenant_id = current_tenant_id().

rules.title: Memory Export Data Portability
rules.description: Users can request exports of their memory data in JSON or CSV format with 24-hour expiry.

rules.title: System Controls Audit Trail
rules.description: All system control changes (arm/disarm) MUST be recorded in immutable audit log with reason, actor, and timestamp.

rules.title: Admin-Only Control Modification
rules.description: Only users with exafy_admin JWT privilege can modify system controls. JWT authentication required.

rules.title: Immutable VTID Specs
rules.description: VTID specs are immutable after lock. Checksum mismatch = hard fail. No VTID may execute without a persisted spec.

rules.title: JWT Authentication on Governance Routes
rules.description: All governance API endpoints require valid Supabase JWT. Write operations require exafy_admin privilege.

rules.title: Fail-Closed Governance Evaluation
rules.description: Governance evaluation must fail-closed. If backend unavailable or errored, deploys are BLOCKED, not silently allowed.
